Title: Docking of acetyl-CoA carboxylase to the plastid envelope membrane attenuates fatty acid production in plants

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: membrane lipid and storage oil production; carbon and energy homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: as part of heteromeric plastid ACCase, catalyzes ATP-dependent carboxylation of acetyl-CoA to form malonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as the first committed and rate-limiting step for plastidial FAS in this paper's framing
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: heteromeric ACCase carboxyltransferase alpha subunit with large noncatalytic C-terminal coiled-coil-containing domain
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: chloroplast inner envelope membrane-associated, stromal side
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light/dark regimes; long day; short day; constant light; extended dark; light recovery
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; microscale thermophoresis; split-luciferase; co-immunoprecipitation; protease protection assay; ACCase activity assay; 14C-acetate labeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Interaction of α-CT with CTIs attenuates htACCase activity and reduces carbon flux into fatty acid synthesis in the light
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The noncatalytic C-terminus of α-CT directly interacts with CTI1, CTI2, and CTI3. 2. α-CT is associated with the chloroplast inner envelope membrane, where CTI docking restrains soluble htACCase activity. 3. Light enhances α-CT–CTI interaction, attenuating fatty acid synthesis and limiting TAG overaccumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating α-CT docking regulators rather than core catalytic abundance may fine-tune plastid fatty acid flux for vegetative oil engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced flux when CTI repression is relieved is associated with altered fatty acid desaturation and, in strong mutant combinations, growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: phosphoproteomic sites discussed from prior studies; no new accession reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study focuses on α-CT as the docking target of CTIs rather than as the genetically perturbed focal enzyme.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports membrane lipid production and storage oil buffering; impacts carbon/energy homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: membrane-associated protein regulator of enzyme complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: small plastidial envelope membrane protein with chloroplast transit peptide, single transmembrane domain, and coiled-coil domain
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: chloroplast inner envelope membrane, stromal-facing C-terminus
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: de novo fatty acid biosynthesis via inhibition of plastid heteromeric ACCase
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction-mediated attenuation of enzyme activity through envelope docking
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: attenuates htACCase activity through direct interaction with α-CT
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Predominant CTI isoform; major negative regulator restraining light-driven fatty acid synthesis and leaf TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: feedforward-like balancing within FAS network because WRI1 activates CTI1 while also activating FAS genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms regulatory CTI/α-CT module at plastid envelope
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: light promotes CTI1–α-CT interaction; WRI1 activates CTI1 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ubiquitous; highest among CTI family; transcript peak at onset of seed maturation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ubiquitous; induced in leaves when WRI1 is overexpressed; present in reproductive organs and embryos
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native ProCTI1; Pro35S:CTI1:YFP/GFP/HA; ProAT2S2:CTI1 seed-specific complementation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light/dark regimes; long day; short day; constant light; extended darkness and light recovery
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; T-DNA null allele; complementation; Y2H; BiFC; MST; split-LUC; co-IP; ACCase assay; 14C-acetate labeling; lipidomics; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS; yeast one-hybrid; transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CTI1 increases soluble htACCase activity, raises fatty acid synthesis, increases leaf total fatty acids and causes strong TAG accumulation in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CTI1 directly binds the noncatalytic C-terminus of α-CT and localizes to the chloroplast inner envelope membrane. 2. cti1 mutants show ~3.5-fold higher soluble ACCase activity and fatty acid synthesis rate, increased leaf fatty acid content, and >4-fold TAG accumulation in leaves. 3. CTI1 is the predominant CTI isoform, likely due to higher α-CT affinity and higher expression. 4. Light strengthens CTI1–α-CT interaction, linking envelope docking to attenuation of FAS. 5. WRI1 directly activates CTI1 promoter activity, coupling fatty acid activation with a braking module.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering vegetative oil crops by reducing CTI1 function to elevate leaf TAG; should consider balancing growth and fatty acid composition
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced C18 desaturation ratio in leaves and seeds; excess vegetative oil may be energetically wasteful; stronger multi-cti combinations reduce growth
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No direct epigenetic mechanism shown; phosphorylation sites on CTI1 C-terminus discussed from prior phosphoproteomics as possible modulators of interaction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CTI1 coevolved with heteromeric ACCase and is absent from grasses lacking htACCase.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: membrane-associated protein regulator of enzyme complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plastid envelope membrane protein with transit peptide, transmembrane domain, and coiled-coil domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: de novo fatty acid biosynthesis via htACCase regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction-mediated attenuation of enzyme activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: attenuates htACCase activity via direct interaction with α-CT
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes redundantly to repression of FAS, but weaker than CTI1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ubiquitous; lower than CTI1; no seed maturation peak reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; Y2H; BiFC; MST; genetic interaction analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes partially redundantly to limiting fatty acid synthesis and TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CTI2 directly interacts with α-CT and localizes with the CTI family at the plastid envelope. 2. Its affinity for α-CT is much lower than CTI1. 3. Single cti2 effects are limited, but combined mutations enhance ACCase activity and FAS.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be stacked with CTI1 manipulation for stronger oil increase, though likely with higher risk of growth defects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contribution to altered desaturation and growth penalty in higher-order mutants
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CTI2/3-like subfamily persists in grasses lacking htACCase, suggesting possible additional functions outside this study.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: membrane-associated protein regulator of enzyme complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plastid envelope membrane protein with transit peptide, transmembrane domain, and coiled-coil domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: de novo fatty acid biosynthesis via htACCase regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction-mediated attenuation of enzyme activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: attenuates htACCase activity via direct interaction with α-CT
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Minor but detectable negative regulator of FAS and TAG accumulation; contributes additively with CTI1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ubiquitous; lower than CTI1; no seed maturation peak reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; Y2H; BiFC; MST; lipidomics; genetic interaction analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CTI3 slightly increases fatty acid synthesis/TAG accumulation and acts additively in double/triple mutants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CTI3 directly interacts with α-CT and localizes to the plastid envelope membrane. 2. cti3 single mutants show modest increases in FAS and TAG accumulation. 3. CTI3 acts partially redundantly with CTI1 and CTI2 to attenuate htACCase.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary engineering target that may augment CTI1-based strategies
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contribution to altered desaturation and growth penalty in higher-order mutants
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Prior phosphoproteomic evidence noted for conserved C-terminal phosphorylation sites; not functionally tested here
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Affinity for α-CT is intermediate between CTI1 and CTI2.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: developing seeds; leaves in overexpression context
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: storage oil accumulation and broader lipid biosynthesis control
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/EREBP family transcription factor recognizing AW box cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of fatty acid biosynthesis and associated regulatory modules
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CTI1 promoter; BCCP2 promoter (positive control in study)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates CTI1 transcription by direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master transcriptional regulator of FAS; in this study, directly induces CTI1, linking activation of lipid synthesis with an attenuation module
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: feedforward balancing circuit because WRI1 activates both FAS genes and CTI1 negative regulator
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: seed maturation/developmental program; overexpression via Pro35Sdual in experiments
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high during seed maturation; absent/reduced in wri1 mutant embryos; ectopically high in overexpression leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: embryos/developing seeds; induced in transgenic leaves when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast one-hybrid; transient transactivation assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter-GUS in WRI1 overexpression and wri1 mutant backgrounds; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CTI1 expression, thereby coupling activation of fatty acid biosynthesis with a brake on ACCase-mediated flux
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRI1 directly binds an AW box in the CTI1 promoter. 2. WRI1 activates CTI1 promoter activity in yeast and planta. 3. CTI1 transcript abundance decreases in wri1 embryos and increases in WRI1-overexpressing leaves. 4. WRI1 therefore positively regulates a negative post-translational regulator of ACCase, suggesting balancing control of FAS.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for designing oil engineering stacks: WRI1-driven enhancement of fatty acid synthesis may simultaneously induce CTI1, so co-manipulation of CTI1 could improve oil gains
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly quantified here for WRI1, but activating CTI1 may dampen maximal FAS output
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: WRI1 is a core subject in Results because the paper experimentally demonstrates direct activation of CTI1 transcription.
